title: Global Styles and Theme Configuration
content:
This file defines the global styles and theme variables for the DocMed application, primarily using Tailwind CSS. ## Purpose - **Global Styling**: Sets up basic HTML and body styles, including smooth scrolling and font rendering. - **Theme Definition**: Defines a comprehensive set of CSS variables (tokens) for colors, borders, shadows, animations, and typography. These variables are designed to be used throughout the application for consistent theming. - **Tailwind CSS Integration**: Imports Tailwind CSS, allowing the use of its utility classes alongside custom CSS. ## Key Features - **CSS Variables (`:root`)**: A wide range of custom properties are defined for various UI elements and states. These are grouped into: - **Core Colors**: `--background`, `--foreground`, `--primary`, `--muted`, `--border`, `--accent`, `--card`, etc. - **Component-Specific**: `--skeleton`, `--btn-border`, `--input`. - **`shadcn/ui` Tokens**: `--ring`, `--popover`, `--secondary`, `--destructive`. - **Layout**: `--radius`. - **Theme Mapping (`@theme inline`)**: Maps the defined CSS variables to standard CSS properties (e.g., `--color-background: hsl(var(--background))`). This allows for easy theming by changing the HSL values of the root variables. - **Animations**: Defines CSS keyframe animations for `ripple` and `orbit` effects, along with corresponding CSS variables (`--animate-ripple`, `--animate-orbit`). - **Shadows**: Defines a custom shadow for input fields (`--shadow-input`). - **Base Styles**: Includes basic resets like `box-sizing: border-box` and sets `html` to use smooth scrolling. The `body` is styled with the defined background, foreground, and font. - **Utility Class**: Defines a custom class `g-button` for consistent button styling. ## Usage Example CSS variables defined in this file can be directly used in other CSS/SCSS files or within inline styles in your components. ```css /* Example in another CSS file */ .my-custom-component { background-color: hsl(var(--card)); color: hsl(var(--popover-foreground)); border-radius: var(--radius); box-shadow: var(--shadow-input); } .ripple-effect { animation: var(--animate-ripple); } ``` ```jsx // Example in a React component
Orbiting Element
```
